project.name: blitzed-out
project.description: A browser-based board game built with React and Firebase that allows players to customize and play a drinking game with real-time multiplayer support. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: useSoundAndDialog
nodes.summary: Hook that watches the latest game message and triggers appropriate audio responses (dice sound, TTS, message sound, haptics) and sets the popup dialog message.

nodes.name: useStatsTracking.ts
nodes.summary: Provides memoized callbacks for recording game statistics events (tile landings, game start, game complete, board categories) to the player stats service.

nodes.name: useStatsTracking
nodes.summary: Hook returning callbacks for trackTileLanding, trackGameComplete, and trackGameStart that silently catch and ignore stats recording errors.

nodes.name: useSubmitGameSettings.ts
nodes.summary: Handles game settings form submission including room change detection, board tile update, settings broadcast, local session creation, and navigation to the game room.

nodes.name: useSubmitGameSettings
nodes.summary: Hook orchestrating the full game settings submission flow: validating room changes, updating tiles and board, broadcasting settings to room participants, initializing local player session, and navigating to the game.

nodes.name: useTTS.ts
nodes.summary: React hook wrapping the TTS (text-to-speech) service, managing playback state (isPlaying, isLoading, error) and exposing speak/stop callbacks with automatic cleanup on unmount.

nodes.name: useTTS
nodes.summary: Exported hook that delegates to the tts service for speech synthesis, managing loading/playing/error state and registering an effect to stop speech on unmount.

nodes.name: useTurnIndicator.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that computes the current turn indicator from the hybrid player list, maintaining a stable turn order and determining next/previous player based on message-driven state changes.

nodes.name: useTurnIndicator
nodes.summary: Default-exported hook computing turn order from hybrid player list, deriving a stable sorted turn order and reacting to message changes to set the current turn indicator.

nodes.name: useUnifiedActionList.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that asynchronously loads and normalizes action groups and intensity tiers from both custom groups and custom tiles stores, returning a unified flat list used by game settings wizards.

nodes.name: useUnifiedActionList
nodes.summary: Default-exported hook querying custom groups and custom tiles stores to build a unified action list with intensity mappings, supporting optional filtering to only groups with tiles.

nodes.name: useUrlImport.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that reads URL query parameters to detect shared game board configurations, imports them into Firebase and the local game board store, and surfaces alert feedback to the user.

nodes.name: useUrlImport
nodes.summary: Default-exported hook parsing URL search params to detect a shared board import, fetching and persisting the board via Firebase and gameBoard store, and notifying the user via alert state.

nodes.name: useWakeLock.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that acquires a Screen Wake Lock when enabled and automatically re-requests it on document visibility change, releasing the lock on cleanup.

nodes.name: useWakeLock
nodes.summary: Exported hook managing the Wake Lock API lifecycle, acquiring a screen lock when enabled and releasing it on unmount or when disabled.

nodes.name: useWizardAnalytics.ts
nodes.summary: Hook providing analytics tracking callbacks (step navigation, funnel step completion) for the game settings wizard, parameterized by game mode and room visibility.

nodes.name: useWizardAnalytics
nodes.summary: Exported hook returning memoized analytics callbacks for tracking wizard step navigation and funnel completion events.

nodes.name: i18n.ts
nodes.summary: Configures and initializes i18next with lazy-loaded locale namespaces, sets up the HTTP backend with a custom request function, and schedules background preloading of common language bundles.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses requestIdleCallback with a setTimeout fallback to preload translations without blocking the main thread.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS stylesheet defining base styles, CSS custom properties, Material-UI overrides, dark-mode theme variables, dice animation keyframes, and layout utilities used across the application.

nodes.name: index.jsx
nodes.summary: Application entry point that bootstraps React with error boundaries, authentication context, i18n, Sentry monitoring, and PWA elements, then mounts the root App component with a smooth loading transition.

nodes.name: initializePWAElements
nodes.summary: Registers Ionic PWA elements (custom web components) for native-like UI on mobile, logging a warning if initialization fails.

nodes.name: loadFontStylesheet
nodes.summary: Dynamically inserts a Google Fonts stylesheet link element into the document head for specified font weights, with error handling.

nodes.name: hideInstantLoading
nodes.summary: Removes the instant-loading splash screen element from the DOM using requestAnimationFrame for a smooth transition after the React tree is mounted.

nodes.name: actionFilesValidation.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite validating all locale action JSON files for correct structure, required fields, consistent placeholder usage, and valid intensity values across all supported languages.

nodes.name: actionParity.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite verifying that each language's action files contain the same number of actions per intensity level as the English reference, ensuring translation parity.

nodes.name: encodingValidation.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite checking that all locale JSON files use valid UTF-8 encoding, have no BOM characters, and contain no invalid Unicode sequences.

nodes.name: fileParity.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite ensuring that all locale directories contain the same set of JSON translation files as the English reference directory, enforcing file-level translation completeness.

nodes.name: actionStringReplacement.ts
nodes.summary: Core service that transforms action text strings by replacing gender-aware anatomy placeholders, role assignments ({dom}, {sub}), and player name tokens with contextually appropriate terms based on locale, player gender, and multiplayer configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses i18next.t for locale-aware anatomy term resolution, and handles both local multiplayer and single-player placeholder pipelines separately.

nodes.name: actionStringReplacement
nodes.summary: Main exported orchestrator that sequentially applies all placeholder replacement passes (generic anatomy, local multiplayer, non-local, capitalization) to produce the final display action string.

nodes.name: determineRoleAssignments
nodes.summary: Determines which local player is assigned as dom and which as sub for a given action, using explicit role mentions or random assignment when ambiguous.

nodes.name: replaceLocalMultiplayerPlaceholders
nodes.summary: Applies the full placeholder replacement pipeline specifically for local multiplayer mode, combining role assignments with anatomy and name substitution.

nodes.name: replaceNonLocalPlaceholders
nodes.summary: Applies placeholder replacement for single-player and online modes, substituting player name, generic role tokens, and anatomy placeholders.

nodes.name: getContextualAnatomyTerm
nodes.summary: Resolves the appropriate anatomy term for a given placeholder type, player gender, role, and action context (penetrative vs. non-penetrative).

nodes.name: replacePipedAnatomyPlaceholders
nodes.summary: Processes piped anatomy placeholders (e.g., {dom|genital}) that target a specific role's anatomy, resolving against the appropriate player's gender.

nodes.name: ambientMusic.ts
nodes.summary: Provides AmbientMusicService for managing looping audio soundscapes via Web Audio API, plus a useAmbientMusic React hook that connects the service to settings store state for enabling/disabling and volume control.

nodes.name: AmbientMusicService
nodes.summary: Singleton service managing Web Audio API soundscape playback, including buffer caching, gain-based volume control, and looping source node lifecycle.

nodes.name: useAmbientMusic
nodes.summary: React hook that syncs the AmbientMusicService singleton with settings store values, exposing callbacks to toggle music, change soundscape, and adjust volume.

nodes.name: analytics.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the AnalyticsService class and singleton instance for tracking user interactions, settings changes, game sessions, and feature usage through Google Analytics 4 (gtag), along with exported event type definitions.

nodes.name: AnalyticsService
nodes.summary: Comprehensive analytics service with 25 typed tracking methods covering game sessions, settings changes, role selection, feature discovery, and funnel steps, dispatched to GA4 via window.gtag.

nodes.name: analyticsTracking.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the AnalyticsTrackingService singleton that wraps the lower-level analytics service and routes settings changes, action selections, custom group usage, intensity patterns, feature discovery, and user flows to the appropriate analytics calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a class-based singleton pattern, exporting a pre-instantiated instance rather than the class itself.

nodes.name: AnalyticsTrackingService
nodes.summary: Singleton service that maps high-level user actions (settings changes, intensity selections, custom groups) to structured analytics events.

nodes.name: anatomyFilterService.ts
nodes.summary: Provides anatomy compatibility filtering functions that determine whether action groups should be shown to a player based on their gender and anatomy requirements, with i18n-aware incompatibility descriptions.

nodes.name: isAnatomyCompatible
nodes.summary: Checks whether a player's gender satisfies an anatomy requirement for a given action.

nodes.name: shouldShowActionGroup
nodes.summary: Determines whether an action group should be visible to a player given their gender and the showIncompatible preference.

nodes.name: getIncompatibilityReason
nodes.summary: Returns a localized explanation of why a gender is incompatible with an anatomy requirement.

nodes.name: anatomyPlaceholderService.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves anatomy placeholder tokens such as {genital}, {hole}, and {chest} in action text by mapping them to locale- and gender-specific terms loaded from i18next translations.

nodes.name: replaceAnatomyPlaceholders
nodes.summary: Replaces all anatomy placeholder tokens in an action string with the correct locale- and role-aware terms.

nodes.name: getAnatomyMappings
nodes.summary: Loads the full anatomy term mappings (keyed by placeholder and locale) for a given gender, falling back to defaults when locale-specific overrides are missing.

nodes.name: buildGame.ts
nodes.summary: Core game-board assembly service that selects, filters, and shuffles tiles from Dexie-backed action groups into a structured board, applying role filtering, intensity progression, and start/finish tiles.

nodes.name: TileShuffleBag
nodes.summary: Implements a shuffle-bag data structure that groups tiles by intensity bucket and refills each bucket with a reshuffled copy when it runs out, ensuring balanced tile distribution.

nodes.name: buildGameBoard
nodes.summary: Main entry point for board generation: loads custom groups and tiles from Dexie, applies role and group filters, then delegates to buildBoard and addStartAndFinishTiles.

nodes.name: buildBoard
nodes.summary: Constructs the main board array by iterating over tile slots, calling buildTileContent and processAppendTiles for each slot.

nodes.name: buildTileContent
nodes.summary: Selects the action text and intensity level for a single board tile from the shuffle bag, respecting user-selected intensity levels.

nodes.name: contentHashing.ts
nodes.summary: Generates and validates SHA-256 content hashes for custom groups, tiles, and disabled-default entries to detect content drift during import/export and migration operations.

nodes.name: generateGroupContentHash
nodes.summary: Produces a deterministic SHA-256 hash of a custom group's name and sorted intensity list for change detection.

nodes.name: generateContentHash
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the appropriate hash generator based on the item type (group, tile, or disabled-default).

nodes.name: validateContentHash
nodes.summary: Recomputes the hash for a given item and compares it against an expected hash, returning a boolean result for integrity verification.

nodes.name: dataCompletenessChecker.ts
nodes.summary: Audits the Dexie database for missing or corrupted action groups by comparing what is stored against the expected action files for a given locale and game mode.

nodes.name: checkDataCompleteness
nodes.summary: Runs a full audit of stored action groups against expected action files, returning a report of missing, corrupted, and extra groups.

nodes.name: isDexieDataComplete
nodes.summary: Returns a boolean indicating whether all expected action groups are present and non-empty in the Dexie store.

nodes.name: dexieActionImport.ts
nodes.summary: Reads custom action groups from the Dexie store and converts them into the key-value actions format consumed by the game board builder.

nodes.name: importActions
nodes.summary: Fetches all available groups for a locale/game-mode from Dexie and returns them in the actions format used by buildGame.

nodes.name: diceBoxPreloader.ts
nodes.summary: Preloads the 3D dice-box-threejs library during browser idle time so it is ready when the player first rolls, avoiding load latency on game start.

nodes.name: firebase.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Firebase service layer providing authentication (anonymous, email, Google), real-time presence management, Firestore board storage, message operations, schedule management, and an internal query-cache/connection-pool layer for performance optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements an in-memory query cache with TTL, priority tiers, debounce, and a connection pool — effectively a mini data-access layer on top of Firestore and Realtime Database.

nodes.name: loginAnonymously
nodes.summary: Signs in a new anonymous Firebase user and sets their display name, with Firefox-mobile-specific error reporting.

nodes.name: setMyPresence
nodes.summary: Manages a user's presence node in Firebase Realtime Database across room transitions, setting onDisconnect cleanup handlers.

nodes.name: getUserList
nodes.summary: Subscribes to the room's user list in Realtime Database, using cache hit detection and debounce to avoid redundant Firebase reads.

nodes.name: sendMessage
nodes.summary: Writes a typed chat or system message document to the Firestore messages collection for the given room.

nodes.name: getMessages
nodes.summary: Subscribes to messages for a room, waiting for auth state before executing the Firestore query to prevent unauthenticated reads.

nodes.name: getOrCreateBoard
nodes.summary: Checks Firestore for an existing board matching the content checksum and returns it, otherwise creates a new board document.

nodes.name: wipeAllAppData
nodes.summary: Performs a full local data purge: signs out, clears localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB, and cookies across all known app domains.

nodes.name: getSchedule
nodes.summary: Subscribes to scheduled game events from Firestore with cache and debounce optimization.

nodes.name: firebaseSignaling.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a WebRTC signaling channel over Firebase Realtime Database, allowing peers in the same room to exchange SDP offers, answers, and ICE candidates for P2P connection setup.

nodes.name: FirebaseSignalingService
nodes.summary: Manages WebRTC signaling state, listening for and sending SDP offers, answers, and ICE candidates through Firebase RTDB paths.

nodes.name: gameSettingsMessage.ts
nodes.summary: Builds and sends a human-readable game-settings summary message to the Firebase chat room, summarizing the active action groups, intensity levels, and custom tile counts.

nodes.name: getSettingsMessage
nodes.summary: Constructs a multi-line i18n-aware message string describing the current game settings, action groups, intensity selections, and custom tile counts.

nodes.name: sendGameSettingsMessage
nodes.summary: Orchestrates board creation and settings message delivery: creates/retrieves a Firebase board, then sends the settings message to the room chat.

nodes.name: getBackgroundSource.ts
nodes.summary: Transforms user-supplied media URLs from a wide range of video and image hosts (Vimeo, YouTube, Imgur, Tenor, etc.) into embeddable or direct-link sources for use as game backgrounds.

nodes.name: processBackground
nodes.summary: Routes a raw URL through host-specific transformers (vimeo, youtube, imgur, etc.) and returns a normalized background source descriptor.

nodes.name: getBackgroundSource
nodes.summary: Main entry point: resolves the background URL from settings or room data and delegates to processBackground, handling public/private room logic.

nodes.name: i18nHelpers.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the supported-languages configuration array (locale codes and display names) used throughout the app for language selection UI and migration logic.

nodes.name: importExport.ts
nodes.summary: Legacy monolithic import/export service that handles serialization, conflict detection, and batched Dexie writes for custom groups, tiles, and disabled-default entries with content hash tracking.

nodes.name: exportAllData
nodes.summary: Exports all custom groups, tiles, and disabled defaults filtered by locale and game mode into a versioned JSON string.

nodes.name: importData
nodes.summary: Parses an import JSON string, validates it, then processes group, tile, and disabled-default imports in order with progress callbacks.

nodes.name: databaseOperations.ts
nodes.summary: Low-level Dexie database utilities for the import/export subsystem: batch fetching, compound-index queries, bulk inserts, conflict detection, and database optimization helpers.

nodes.name: batchInsertTiles
nodes.summary: Inserts tiles in chunked Dexie transactions, falling back to individual adds when bulkAdd encounters constraint violations.

nodes.name: buildGroupMappingContext
nodes.summary: Fetches all groups for a locale/game-mode and builds a name-to-id mapping context used by the import service.

nodes.name: exportService.ts
nodes.summary: Optimized export service that streams tiles in chunks per group to avoid memory overloads, building a structured export payload with group metadata and intensity-keyed tile arrays.

nodes.name: exportData
nodes.summary: Main export function: fetches groups, processes tiles in streaming batches, cleans up empty groups, and serializes the result to JSON.

nodes.name: getExportableGroupStats
nodes.summary: Computes per-group export statistics (custom tile count, disabled-default count) for display in the export UI.

nodes.name: importService.ts
nodes.summary: Optimized import service that parses, validates, and writes custom group and tile data into Dexie with conflict handling, progress reporting, and post-import database optimization.

nodes.name: importData
nodes.summary: Entry point for the optimized import flow: parses JSON, validates structure, maps groups, inserts tiles, then optimizes the database.

nodes.name: importGroups
nodes.summary: Iterates imported group definitions, validates each, and batch-inserts new groups while building the group-mapping context for tile import.

nodes.name: importTiles
nodes.summary: Processes all tiles from the import data grouped by group name, performing conflict detection and batch inserting resolved tiles.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Public API barrel for the importExport subsystem, re-exporting types and wrapping exportData/importData with convenience aliases (exportCleanData, importCleanData) plus utility helpers for size estimation, format validation, and progress tracking.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Developer documentation for the import/export performance optimization subsystem, covering the problems solved, module architecture, key optimizations, usage examples, database index requirements, migration guide, and testing recommendations.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines TypeScript interfaces and constants for the import/export subsystem including ImportResult, ImportOptions, ExportOptions, ImportProgress, CleanExportData, and EXPORT_FORMAT_VERSION.

nodes.name: localPlayerService.ts
nodes.summary: Manages local (offline/hot-seat) multiplayer sessions stored in Dexie, including session creation, turn advancement, player position tracking, and session validation.

nodes.name: LocalPlayerService
nodes.summary: Singleton service managing the lifecycle of local multiplayer game sessions persisted in Dexie, including turn rotation and player position updates.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Exports migration-related constants including supported language codes, game modes, localStorage keys for migration state, and timeout/delay values used by the migration system.

nodes.name: errorHandling.ts
nodes.summary: Provides structured error logging, a safe localStorage wrapper, a generic error-handling HOF, and a duplicate-detection helper for the migration subsystem.

nodes.name: withErrorHandling
nodes.summary: Wraps an async operation to catch errors, log them via logError, and return a provided fallback value, preventing unhandled rejections in migration flows.

nodes.name: fileDiscovery.ts
nodes.summary: Detects the current app language from localStorage or browser navigator, and dynamically discovers available action-group names for a locale/game-mode by lazy-importing the action bundle.

nodes.name: getActionGroupNames
nodes.summary: Dynamically imports the action bundle for a locale/game-mode and returns the sorted list of top-level group names.

nodes.name: groupIdMigration.ts
nodes.summary: Handles migration of legacy auto-increment group IDs to deterministic content-based IDs in Dexie, including auditing, resolution, validation, and the full migration orchestration function.

nodes.name: createDeterministicGroupId
nodes.summary: Generates a stable group ID string from group name, locale, and game mode using a djb2-style hash, ensuring IDs are reproducible across import/export cycles.

nodes.name: runFullGroupIdMigration
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the complete group ID migration pipeline: audit, migrate defaults, migrate custom IDs, then validate integrity.

nodes.name: migrateGroupIds
nodes.summary: Processes tiles with missing or blank group_id values in batches, logging orphaned tiles and running a post-migration audit.

nodes.name: importOperations.ts
nodes.summary: Handles the core import logic for migrating action group files into the local Dexie database, including retry logic, duplicate detection, and per-locale/game-mode group processing.

nodes.name: importActionFile
nodes.summary: Dynamically imports an action file for a given group, locale, and game mode, then transforms and collects its tiles into custom groups and tiles.

nodes.name: importGroupsForLocaleAndGameMode
nodes.summary: Orchestrates importing all action groups for a given locale and game mode, updating or creating groups and tiles in a database transaction.

nodes.name: cleanupDuplicateGroups
nodes.summary: Removes duplicate custom groups from the database, ensuring data integrity after migration.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Main entry point for the migration module, orchestrating full and partial language migrations, background queuing, version checks, and status reporting.

nodes.name: migrateCurrentLanguage
nodes.summary: Migrates action groups for the user's current locale, handling in-progress checks and marking completion on success.

nodes.name: migrateRemainingLanguages
nodes.summary: Background-migrates all locales except the currently active one, pacing imports with delays and marking overall completion when all languages finish.

nodes.name: runMigrationIfNeeded
nodes.summary: Top-level migration entry: checks version changes, in-progress flags, and completion state before triggering current-language migration and background queuing.

nodes.name: ensureLanguageMigrated
nodes.summary: Ensures a specific locale is migrated, waiting for any in-progress migration or triggering a fresh one if needed.

nodes.name: statusManager.ts

nodes.summary: Primary Vitest configuration for local development that merges with the full Vite config, enabling CSS processing, coverage reporters, and slow test threshold detection.

layers.name: Application Entry
layers.description: Root application bootstrap files including the React entry point, global CSS, theme configuration, and i18n initialization for the Blitzed Out adult party game app.

layers.name: UI Views & Components
layers.description: Page-level views (Room, GameSettings, GameSettingsWizard, Navigation, etc.) and reusable UI components (VideoCall, MessageList, auth, etc.) built with React and Material-UI v7.

layers.name: State Management
layers.description: Zustand stores for game state (settings, custom tiles/groups, game board, dice, messages, video call) and React context providers for auth, messages, scheduling, theming, and user migration.

layers.name: Custom Hooks
layers.description: Custom React hooks encapsulating game logic, player management, Firebase interactions, audio/TTS, analytics, and UI behaviors consumed by views and components.

layers.name: Services & Backend
layers.description: Business logic services (Firebase sync, game building, import/export, migration, signaling, analytics, TTS) and Firebase Cloud Functions for server-side operations like stale user cleanup.

layers.name: Types & Constants
layers.description: TypeScript type definitions and interfaces for all domain entities (settings, custom tiles, game board, players, messages, sync), action constants, and WebRTC configuration.

layers.name: Utilities & Helpers
layers.description: Pure utility functions for string manipulation, URL handling, audio context, browser detection, haptics, database recovery, lazy loading, and message formatting used across the codebase.

layers.name: Localization & Assets
layers.description: i18next translation JSON files for five languages (en/es/fr/zh/hi) covering local and online game action categories, anatomy terms, and UI strings, plus ambient sound directory metadata.

layers.name: Tests & Mocks
layers.description: Vitest unit and integration tests, test utilities, fixtures, and mock implementations for Firebase, hooks, media devices, and PeerJS across all feature areas of the codebase.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
layers.description: Project-level configuration files (TypeScript, Vite, ESLint, Firebase rules/indexes), public static assets, build/validation scripts, CI/CD pipeline, Git hooks, and project documentation.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and CLAUDE.md to orient yourself: Blitzed Out is an adult party game where players roll dice to receive actions from a customizable game board, with support for solo, local multiplayer, and online multiplayer modes. CLAUDE.md is the developer playbook — it specifies the tech stack (React 19, MUI v7, Zustand, Dexie, Firebase, i18next), the TDD workflow, memory-safe test commands, and internationalization rules that govern every contribution. Together these two documents provide the map for the entire tour ahead.

tour.title: App Bootstrap
tour.description: The real entry point is `src/index.jsx`, which mounts the React tree with error boundaries, authentication context, i18n initialization, Sentry monitoring, and PWA support before rendering the root `App.tsx`. `App.tsx` is intentionally thin: it calls `useMinimalAuth` and either renders a loading skeleton or the full application, keeping the top-level component focused on a single concern. This two-file split — a bootstrapping outer shell and a minimal root component — is a pattern that recurs throughout the codebase.
tour.languageLesson: React 19 uses `createRoot` instead of `ReactDOM.render`. Wrapping the root in an `ErrorBoundary` catches rendering crashes in the entire subtree without bringing down the whole page.

tour.title: Theme & Localization
tour.description: `theme.ts` defines both a light and dark Material-UI theme using `getThemeByMode`, with extensive component overrides that enforce the dark-first aesthetic across the entire UI — avoiding hard-coded light colors like `grey.50` is a project coding standard. `i18n.ts` configures i18next with lazy-loaded locale namespaces and a custom HTTP backend, supporting five languages (en, es, fr, zh, hi) that are preloaded in the background after initial paint. These two files establish the visual and language foundations that every component builds on.
tour.languageLesson: i18next namespace lazy-loading splits translation files so only the current language is fetched on startup. Background preloading (`preload`) then fetches the remaining namespaces during idle time, balancing fast first-load with instant language-switching.

tour.title: Core Domain Types
tour.description: `src/types/` is the vocabulary of the entire application — `Settings.ts` (56 fan-in) is the single most-imported file in the codebase, defining the game configuration shape shared between the Zustand store and Firebase sync. `customGroups.ts` and `customTiles.ts` model the player-configurable content system (activity categories and individual action tiles), while `gameBoard.ts` defines the board cell structures that drive the visual game board. `types/index.ts` is a barrel re-exporting all of these, giving every consumer a single import point.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript barrel files (`export * from`) create a single public API surface for a module. Consumers import from the barrel (`@/types`) rather than deep paths, making internal restructuring transparent to callers.

tour.title: Local Persistence (Dexie / IndexedDB)
tour.description: `stores/store.ts` defines `BlitzedOutDatabase`, a Dexie class with versioned schema migrations covering all IndexedDB tables (customGroups, customTiles, gameBoards, and more). The three sibling files — `customGroups.ts`, `customTiles.ts`, and `gameBoard.ts` in `src/stores/` — are the data-access layer for those tables, each exporting 15–20 functions for CRUD, filtering, pagination, import/export, and cross-table joins. This layer is what allows the game to work fully offline: all player-created content lives in IndexedDB first, and Firebase sync is layered on top.
tour.languageLesson: Dexie wraps the browser's IndexedDB API with a promise-based interface and automatic versioned migrations. The `version(n).stores({...})` chain is declarative: Dexie computes the diff and applies schema upgrades when a user opens the app with a newer version.

tour.title: Zustand Settings Store
tour.description: `settingsStore.ts` is the second-most-imported file in the codebase (33 fan-in) and acts as the runtime state bridge between the UI, the game engine, and Firebase. It exposes the full `Settings` shape through `useSettingsStore`, plus two convenience selector hooks — `useSettings` for app-level config and `useGameSettings` for game-specific config — so components subscribe only to the slices they need. Understanding what lives in this store vs. Dexie is key: Zustand holds the current session's active settings in memory, while Dexie persists them to disk.

tour.title: Firebase Service Layer
tour.description: `services/firebase.ts` is the central Firebase integration, handling anonymous/email/Google authentication, real-time presence, Firestore board storage, in-game messaging, and schedule management — all from a single file with 21 fan-in. The companion config files tell the full deployment picture: `firebase.json` configures Hosting with strict CSP/HSTS security headers and asset caching rules, while `database.rules.json` defines authentication-gated read/write access rules for the Realtime Database paths used by rooms and video calls.
tour.languageLesson: Firebase Realtime Database rules are evaluated top-down: a `.read` or `.write` at a parent path cascades to children unless a child rule overrides it. Keeping rules tight (require `auth != null`) prevents unauthenticated bulk reads even if the client-side code has a bug.

tour.title: Firebase Sync Subsystem
tour.description: The `src/services/sync/` directory implements a structured sync pattern: `base.ts` is an abstract class providing shared Firebase auth retrieval, Firestore access helpers, and standardized result shapes; each entity-specific class (`customGroupsSync.ts`, `settingsSync.ts`, etc.) extends it to handle one IndexedDB table; and `syncOrchestrator.ts` calls them in sequence and aggregates results. `sync/index.ts` is the barrel re-exporting the whole subsystem. This fan-out from a single orchestrator means sync behavior is easy to test and extend one entity at a time.
tour.languageLesson: The abstract-base-class pattern in TypeScript enforces a contract: subclasses must implement entity-specific methods while inheriting shared infrastructure. Returning standardized `{success, error}` shapes from every sync method makes the orchestrator's aggregation logic uniform and the results easy to surface in the UI.

tour.title: Migration System
tour.description: When a player switches languages, the game's action text must be re-migrated from the new locale's JSON bundles into the local IndexedDB. `context/migration.tsx` is a React context provider that coordinates this: it performs health checks, integrity verification, and recovery, then exposes `ensureLanguageMigrated` to consumers. `services/migration/index.ts` does the heavy lifting — orchestrating full and partial migrations, background queuing, version checks, and status reporting. This system is what allows Blitzed Out to support 5 languages without requiring a server-side database per locale.

tour.title: Game Setup Wizard
tour.description: The `GameSettingsWizard` is a newcomer's first substantive interaction with the app: it walks through game mode selection, local player configuration, optional room setup, action tile selection, and a finish screen. `useSubmitGameSettings.ts` handles the form submission logic — detecting room changes, updating board tiles, broadcasting settings to remote players via Firebase, and navigating to the game room. The wizard's fan-out of 15 imports reflects how much of the system it must coordinate: types, stores, Firebase, and routing all converge here.

tour.title: Player Model
tour.description: Blitzed Out supports three player configurations: local-only players stored in IndexedDB, remote players synced via Firebase, and a hybrid mode that merges both. `useLocalPlayers.ts` manages offline session init, navigation, and position tracking. `useAuth.ts` provides the authentication context that distinguishes local from remote sessions. `useHybridPlayerList.ts` merges these two data sources into a single unified player list using type guard helpers, which is what the Room view consumes to render all player tokens regardless of their origin.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript type guard functions (`function isLocalPlayer(p): p is LocalPlayer`) narrow union types at runtime, letting the compiler enforce branch-specific property access. This pattern removes the need for ad-hoc casting throughout the codebase.

tour.title: Room & Gameplay Loop
tour.description: `views/Room/index.tsx` is the top-level game session component with the widest fan-out in the codebase (34 imports), orchestrating player presence, turn transitions, dice rolls, background rendering, video calls, and keyboard shortcuts. `RollButton/index.tsx` handles the roll computation, countdown timer, analytics, and haptic feedback, exposing a `triggerRoll` ref for keyboard shortcuts. `GameBoard/index.tsx` renders the tile sequence, animates player tokens to their current positions, and auto-scrolls to keep the active player visible. Together, these three files are the beating heart of the gameplay experience.
tour.languageLesson: React's `useImperativeHandle` + `forwardRef` lets a parent component call imperative methods (like `triggerRoll`) on a child without lifting all state up. This is preferable to lifting state when the child owns complex internal timing logic that callers should not need to understand.

tour.title: Chromecast / Secondary Screen
tour.description: `views/Cast/index.tsx` is the Cast receiver view that streams the game board to a Chromecast or secondary display: it authenticates anonymously with Firebase, unlocks audio autoplay, manages fullscreen, and subscribes to real-time message updates so the cast screen stays synchronized with the room. This component re-uses the same `GameBoard` component from Step 12 but in a read-only context, demonstrating how the component architecture supports both interactive and display-only rendering from a single implementation.

tour.title: CI/CD Pipeline
tour.description: The GitHub Actions workflow in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs three sequential jobs — lint, test, and build — on every push and pull request to `dev` and `master`. It uses Node.js 24 with Vitest result caching to keep test runs fast. This pipeline enforces the same quality gates described in CLAUDE.md (ESLint + type-check + Vitest) automatically on every contribution, ensuring that the TDD discipline from Step 1's developer playbook is machine-enforced and not just advisory.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions jobs run in parallel by default; adding `needs: [lint]` makes a job wait for its dependency. Sequential job ordering here is intentional: there is no point running an expensive Vitest suite if the linter already found a syntax error.
